item_name: INSTALL FLASHING AND SEAL VENT STACK

description:
Removal and disposal of 4 feet of existing drywall panels from the walls.
Inspection of framing and insulation for moisture damage

Installation of vapor barrier or moisture membrane (if not already present or if damaged)
Treatment of framing with anti-microbial solution (if necessary)

Installation of cementitious backer board panels
Proper fastening with corrosion-resistant screws
Sealing of joints using alkali-resistant fiberglass mesh tape
Application of thin-set mortar or an app

Application of a liquid waterproofing membrane over cement board
This additional waterproofing layer provides enhanced protection against vapor migration and water intrusion.
Moisture levels must be checked — excessive moisture can cause blistering and delamination

Remove dust, oil, grease, chemical residues, and contaminants.
Use appropriate degreasers if necessary

Rinse thoroughly and allow complete drying

Perform heavy grinding, diamond grinding, or shot blasting.
Objective: open the concrete pores and create a surface profile for mechanical bonding.
The surface should have a rough texture similar to c

Cracks must be opened and treated with epoxy filler or repair mortar.
Holes and surface defects must be leveled

Apply a primer compatible with the selected system.
The primer penetrates the pores and promotes chemical bonding between the concrete and coating

Apply according to the specified thickness (typically 2 to 4 mm for technical areas).
For severe chemical environments, a 2-coat system is recommended.

Shut down the system
Drain tank and piping
Disconnect the pump and filter
Cut and cap supply/return lines
Remove tank and equipment (leave onsite)

Reset the tank on the stand
Reinstall the pump and filter
Reconnect all PVC piping
Replace unions, fittings, and valves as required
Pressure test and leak check
Startup and operational check
